title: Interrupt-tier is an immediate ping, not a queue block; the phase GATE is the backstop
context: HDR-010 defines tier-1 'interrupt' as interrupt-blocking, and its amendment says the judge can 'pause dependents on a call that should have blocked.' Implementing a true queue block requires a third verify disposition (verdict: escalate), a result-schema bump (0.2.0 -> 0.3.0), and an _apply_verdict branch routing the task to paused_for_human. The leanness review quantified the exposure: a successful task sits in awaiting_verification (its dependents cannot claim) until the verifier passes, and the phase GATE already forces the operator to review every pending AgDR before the next phase runs. So a true block only fences same-phase siblings for the window between the AgDR and the gate.

options.name: true-block
options.rationale: Route interrupt-tier to paused_for_human, holding the phase open until sb resolve; faithful to HDR-010's literal tier-1.
options.tradeoff: result-schema bump + _apply_verdict branch + block() and fixture updates, to fence only same-phase siblings over a bounded, revertible window.

options.name: immediate-ping
options.rationale: Interrupt fires an immediate sb notify (now, not at the next digest cycle); the task still proceeds to done. Three tiers become three notify cadences: immediate / digest / gate-only. Zero engine or result-schema change.
options.tradeoff: Softens HDR-010's literal tier-1 from 'blocks the queue' to 'forces immediate attention'; relies on the phase GATE as the backstop and pre-merge revert (HDR-010 tier-2's own escape hatch) for the same-phase-sibling window.

reasoning: The dangerous compounding case is already serialized by the verification lane and the phase gate; a true block adds engine + schema surface to cover only same-phase siblings, which HDR-010 tier-2 was explicitly designed to tolerate (work proceeds, overturn bounded by revert). An immediate ping preserves a meaningful three-way distinction (immediate / digest / silent) at zero engine cost. Revisit the true-block only if the phase-gate backstop proves inadequate in practice.

steelman.strongest_case: A worker that proceeded on a call it should have stopped on can compound real damage among same-phase siblings before the gate; only a hard pause GUARANTEES nothing further runs on that path, which is what HDR-010 literally asked for.

blast_radius: Notify/digest routing only; no engine task-routing or schema change. Reversible: the true-block path can be added later behind the same tag if needed.

feedback.note: Chose immediate-ping over true-block in the C design session (2026-06-23): the phase gate is the real backstop and the engine/schema cost of a true block is not worth fencing only same-phase siblings. Formal approval at the gate.

Formal approval at the Plan 3-C gate (PR #5 director stamp). Re-affirmed after re-reading the tradeoff: tier-1 softens to an immediate ping, the phase GATE is the accepted backstop, same-phase-sibling exposure is bounded and revertible pre-merge.
